title: Restaurant manager
summary: A leadership role responsible for daily restaurant operations, team coordination, service quality, and guest satisfaction.
responsibilities: Coordinate daily restaurant operations and service flow
Support, train, and supervise front-of-house staff
Handle guest feedback and resolve service issues
Control reservations, table planning, and event preparation
Monitor service standards, cleanliness, and team discipline
requirements: Experience in restaurant or hotel service management
Strong communication and leadership skills
Ability to work under pressure during busy shifts
Understanding of hospitality standards and guest care
benefits: Team meals or staff discounts may be available
Supportive team and clear work processes
body: The restaurant manager keeps the service organized from opening to closing. This role connects guests, waiters, kitchen staff, bar staff, and owners into one clear daily workflow.
The position suits someone who can lead calmly, notice details, and keep the restaurant atmosphere professional even during busy hours.
A good manager improves both guest experience and team confidence by setting clear standards and helping people work together.
highlights: Maintain service quality during busy shifts

title: Front desk receptionist
summary: A guest-facing hotel role focused on check-in, check-out, reservations, calls, and first-contact hospitality.
responsibilities: Welcome guests and manage check-in and check-out
Process room reservations and guest requests
Answer phone calls, messages, and basic inquiries
Coordinate with housekeeping and restaurant teams
Keep reception information accurate and organized
requirements: Polite communication and attention to detail
Basic computer skills
Ability to handle guest questions calmly
English or other foreign languages are a plus
benefits: Hospitality experience and guest communication practice
Growth into hotel administration or guest relations
body: The receptionist is often the first person a hotel guest meets. This role shapes the first impression through clear communication, friendly attitude, and organized service.
The position includes reservations, check-in, check-out, calls, messages, and coordination with other departments.
It is a good role for someone who enjoys communication and can keep information accurate during active working hours.
highlights: Communicate with guests before and during stays

title: Waiter
summary: A front-of-house service role focused on welcoming guests, taking orders, serving dishes, and keeping the table experience smooth.
responsibilities: Welcome guests and help them choose from the menu
Take food and drink orders accurately
Serve dishes and drinks according to service standards
Keep tables clean and ready for the next guests
Communicate guest requests to the kitchen and bar
requirements: Friendly communication and neat appearance
Ability to work on feet during active shifts
Attention to orders, timing, and table details
Teamwork with kitchen, bar, and floor staff
benefits: Flexible shift options may be available
Growth into senior waiter or shift supervisor
body: The waiter supports the main guest experience in the restaurant. Good service depends on timing, attention, polite communication, and teamwork.
This role is suitable for active people who enjoy working with guests and can stay organized during busy service.
Experience is helpful, but many businesses can train motivated candidates who are ready to learn service standards.
highlights: Serve guests during breakfast, lunch, or dinner
Take accurate food and drink orders
Work closely with kitchen and bar teams

title: Bartender
summary: A bar role responsible for preparing drinks, keeping the bar organized, supporting service, and creating a friendly guest experience.
responsibilities: Prepare alcoholic and non-alcoholic drinks
Keep the bar clean, stocked, and organized
Support waiters with drink orders
Control bar inventory and report missing items
Communicate with guests at the bar politely and professionally
requirements: Knowledge of basic cocktails and bar service
Responsible attitude toward alcohol service
Accuracy, cleanliness, and speed
Ability to work evening and weekend shifts
benefits: Opportunity to develop cocktail and bar skills
Growth into head bartender or bar manager
body: The bartender keeps the drink service fast, clean, and consistent. This role supports both guests at the bar and waiters working on the floor.
A strong bartender understands recipes, presentation, stock control, and responsible alcohol service.
The position works well for someone who enjoys active evening service and can stay focused when multiple orders arrive at once.
highlights: Prepare cocktails, wine, beer, and soft drinks
Keep the bar clean and stocked
Support restaurant service during busy hours

title: Barista
summary: A coffee-focused role responsible for preparing espresso drinks, tea, simple desserts, and friendly cafe service.
responsibilities: Prepare espresso, cappuccino, latte, tea, and seasonal drinks
Serve desserts and simple cafe items
Keep the coffee station clean and organized
Support takeaway and dine-in coffee orders
Maintain friendly communication with guests
requirements: Interest in coffee and guest service
Accuracy with recipes and drink presentation
Cleanliness and attention to station setup
Ability to work morning or daytime shifts
benefits: Growth into senior barista or cafe supervisor
body: The barista creates small daily moments for guests through coffee, tea, desserts, and friendly service.
This role requires attention to taste, presentation, cleanliness, and timing, especially during morning and afternoon rushes.
It is a good fit for someone who likes coffee culture and wants to grow in cafe or restaurant service.

title: Chef
summary: A kitchen role focused on preparing dishes, organizing production, maintaining quality, and supporting daily service.
responsibilities: Prepare dishes according to recipes and service standards
Organize ingredients, prep work, and station readiness
Control taste, portioning, and presentation
Keep kitchen areas clean and safe
Support menu updates and seasonal specials
requirements: Kitchen experience in restaurant, hotel, or catering
Understanding of food safety and cleanliness
Ability to work during active service
Consistency in taste, speed, and presentation
benefits: Opportunity to work with seasonal menus
Growth into sous chef or head chef
Team meals or staff discounts may be available
body: The chef is responsible for consistent food quality during daily service. This includes preparation, cooking, plating, cleanliness, and timing.
The role suits candidates who can follow standards, work quickly, and stay focused while coordinating with the kitchen team.
A strong chef helps the restaurant keep the menu reliable and guests satisfied.

title: Kitchen assistant
summary: A support kitchen role focused on preparation, cleaning, simple tasks, and helping chefs during service.
responsibilities: Help with washing, cutting, and basic food preparation
Keep kitchen tools and work areas clean
Support chefs during busy service
Organize ingredients and simple supplies
Follow hygiene and safety rules
requirements: Willingness to learn kitchen work
Cleanliness and responsible attitude
Ability to follow instructions
Readiness for active physical work
benefits: Possible growth into cook or chef assistant
body: The kitchen assistant helps the kitchen run smoothly by handling preparation, cleaning, and simple support tasks.
This role is a good entry point for people who want to learn how restaurant kitchens work.
Reliability, cleanliness, and readiness to help are more important than previous experience.
highlights: Good entry point into restaurant work

title: Housekeeper
summary: A hotel cleanliness role focused on room preparation, guest comfort, laundry coordination, and public area care.
responsibilities: Clean and prepare hotel rooms for guest arrival
Change bed linen, towels, and room supplies
Report maintenance issues or missing items
Keep public hotel areas clean and tidy
Coordinate with reception about room readiness
requirements: Attention to cleanliness and details
Responsible attitude toward guest property
Ability to follow room preparation standards
Readiness for active physical work
benefits: Growth into senior housekeeper or housekeeping supervisor
body: The housekeeper plays a key role in hotel comfort. Clean rooms, fresh linen, and prepared guest spaces are essential for a good stay.
This role requires accuracy, responsibility, and attention to details that guests immediately notice.
Housekeeping works closely with reception to keep room availability clear and guest arrivals smooth.
highlights: Keep hotel spaces clean and comfortable

title: Dishwasher
summary: A support role responsible for clean dishes, kitchen tools, and basic hygiene flow during restaurant service.
responsibilities: Wash dishes, glasses, cutlery, and kitchen tools
Keep dishwashing area clean and organized
Support kitchen and service teams with clean equipment
Sort waste and follow hygiene rules
Help with simple cleaning tasks when needed
requirements: Responsible and punctual attitude
Readiness for active physical work
Cleanliness and attention to hygiene
Ability to work during busy service
body: The dishwasher keeps the service moving by making sure dishes, glasses, and kitchen tools are clean and ready.
This role is essential during busy restaurant hours because both kitchen and waiters depend on clean equipment.
It is a good starting position for people who want simple, stable work in hospitality.

title: Event coordinator
summary: A coordination role focused on banquets, birthdays, weddings, group bookings, timing, and guest communication.
responsibilities: Process event inquiries and prepare basic offers
Coordinate menus, timing, seating, and guest needs
Communicate with kitchen, service, and management teams
Support event setup and on-site coordination
Follow up with guests before and after events
requirements: Strong communication and organization
Experience with guests, events, or hospitality service
Ability to manage details and timing
Calm attitude during active event preparation
benefits: Experience with weddings, banquets, and business events
Dynamic work with different guest formats
body: The event coordinator helps guests turn an inquiry into a clear event plan. This includes communication, menu details, seating, timing, and coordination with the team.
The role is important for birthdays, banquets, weddings, conferences, and group dinners.
A strong coordinator keeps details organized so guests feel confident before the event begins.

title: Spa attendant
summary: A wellness area role focused on guest welcome, spa cleanliness, towels, time slots, and calm service flow.
responsibilities: Welcome spa guests and explain basic rules
Prepare towels, rest areas, and guest supplies
Monitor cleanliness and comfort in spa zones
Coordinate time slots and guest flow
Report technical or maintenance issues
requirements: Polite and calm communication
Attention to cleanliness and safety
Responsible attitude toward guest comfort
Readiness to follow spa rules and procedures
benefits: Growth into spa administrator or supervisor
body: The spa attendant keeps the wellness area clean, calm, and ready for guests.
This role includes guest welcome, towels, rest spaces, timing, and basic rule explanation.
It is a good fit for someone who enjoys quiet service, cleanliness, and organized guest support.
highlights: Support sauna, hammam, or relax zone guests
Keep spa areas clean and ready
Coordinate simple guest flow and time slots

title: Maintenance technician
summary: A technical support role focused on repairs, equipment checks, lighting, plumbing, furniture, and daily property upkeep.
responsibilities: Handle minor repairs around the property
Check lighting, plumbing, furniture, and equipment
Respond to urgent maintenance requests
Support safe operation of hotel, restaurant, and spa areas
Track repair needs and report larger issues
requirements: Practical repair and technical skills
Responsible attitude toward safety
Ability to react quickly to issues
Experience with building maintenance is preferred
benefits: Important role in guest comfort and safety
body: The maintenance technician helps the business stay safe, functional, and comfortable for guests and staff.
The role includes small repairs, equipment checks, urgent fixes, and regular property care.
Good maintenance prevents service interruptions and helps every department work smoothly.
highlights: Keep guest areas safe and functional
